title: Docker command 'start' not found error
symptom: When trying to start the n8n Docker container, the error 'Command "start" not found' is displayed, causing the container to stop running.
root_cause: The command 'n8n start' is incorrect for running the n8n Docker container. The correct command should simply be 'n8n' without 'start'. This confusion can arise from misinterpretation of Docker commands or YML configurations.
fix_steps: Remove 'start' from the command, leaving just 'n8n'
Save the changes and restart the Docker container
agent_instruction: Edit the Docker configuration to use 'n8n' instead of 'n8n start'.
